title: Hopf Algebras in the Cohomology of A_g
speaker: Melody Chan

proof_stages.label: Tropical boundary complexes detect weight-zero cohomology
proof_stages.summary: The talk begins from the principle that normal-crossings compactifications have tropical or dual cone complexes whose compactly supported cohomology injects canonically into the compactly supported cohomology of the original complex variety as Deligne's weight-zero subspace.

proof_stages.label: Moduli spaces as the geometric targets
proof_stages.summary: The central varieties are introduced as moduli spaces, especially A_g of principally polarized abelian varieties and M_g of curves, with their interpretation as rational classifying spaces for arithmetic or mapping-class groups.

proof_stages.label: Curve-side tropicalization and graph-complex cohomology
proof_stages.summary: The Deligne-Mumford compactification of M_g has boundary strata governed by stable graphs, so M_g^trop is a cone complex of metric stable graphs and its compactly supported cohomology identifies, up to shifts and duals, with Kontsevich's commutative graph complex, supplying graph-complex and Grothendieck-Teichmuller classes.

proof_stages.label: Tropical A_g and perfect quadratic form combinatorics
proof_stages.summary: The abelian-variety side is described via Siegel upper half-space, toroidal compactifications, the tropical moduli space A_g^trop, and its combinatorics of admissible decompositions and perfect quadratic forms, with the tropical Schottky locus governed by regular matroids.

proof_stages.label: Graph K-theory space and graph spectral sequence
proof_stages.summary: A coarse K-theory space of all metric graphs is constructed from cubes or equivalently from a simplicial groupoid of graph diagrams, filtered by first Betti number, and its associated graph spectral sequence carries a commutative bi-algebra structure related to the Connes-Kreimer coproduct.

proof_stages.label: Quillen K-theory and tropical Torelli comparison
proof_stages.summary: Replacing graphs by finitely generated projective modules recovers Quillen's K-theory construction, the graph-to-module map given by first homology is compatible with the tropical Torelli map on filtered quotients, and Quillen's spectral sequence of Hopf algebras transfers the needed Hopf-algebraic structure to the A_g setting.

proof_stages.label: Hopf algebra theorem and exponential-growth consequences
proof_stages.summary: The total weight-zero compactly supported cohomology of all A_g is stated to form a bigraded cocommutative Hopf algebra, whose primitives contain a large free Lie algebra detected by invariant differential-form integrals and wheel/graph-complex classes; Milnor-Moore then yields exponential families of cohomology classes and applications to unstable cohomology of GL_g(Z) and SL_g(Z).

examples.name: The torus C*^2 compactified in P^2
examples.note: The algebraic torus with the three coordinate boundary lines in P^2 is used as a low-dimensional cartoon to make the boundary-strata poset, dual cone complex, and resulting weight-zero class visible.
examples.engagement_reason: She develops the torus-in-P2 cartoon by listing boundary divisors and strata, building the dual cone complex, compactifying it, and reading off a cohomology class.

examples.name: Genus-two stable curve and dual stable graph
